properties.remark: EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED DOWNED TREES AND POWERLINES ALONG THE INTERSTATE 10 CORRIDOR FROM MILE MARKER 298 TO MILE MARKER 301. THIS IS JUST EAST OF THE I-75/I-10 INTERCHANGE AND NEAR THE U.S. 41 HIGHWAY EXIT ON I-10. DAMAGE ESTIMATE IS PRELIMINARY AND TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR IMAGERY.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED TREE DOWN ON A HOME ON NORTHWEST MOORE ROAD. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED. TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

STORM SURVEY INDICATED A VERY BRIEF EF0 TORNADO WITH WINDS UP TO 80 MPH PRODUCED A QUARTER MILE OF DAMAGE THAT EXTENDED FROM NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF 96TH PLACE AND 147TH ROAD TOWARD THE EAST ALONG 96TH PLACE. SEVERAL PROPERTIES HAD DAMAGE INCLUDING ROOF DAMAGE. THERE WAS EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE WITH VERY LARGE TREES INCLUDING LIVE AND WATER OAKS AS WELL AS LARGE DIAMETER PINE TREES TWISTED...BLOWN DOWN AND SNAPPED. PATH LENGTH 0.25 MILE. PATH WIDTH ABOUT 50 YARDS.

AN EF-0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN AT HUGUENOT PARK BEACH FROM 0138 PM TO 0141 PM EDT. ESTIMATED WINDS OF 65 MPH AND A PATH LENGTH OF 0.1 MILES AND WIDTH OF 40 YARDS. THIS TORNADO PASSED THE NORTHERN SIDE OF CASTILLO DE SAN MARCOS UPROOTING 2 TREES BEFORE STRENGTHENING TO EF-1 AND MOVING INTO THE HUGUENOT CEMETERY. MULTIPLE TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED IN THE CEMETERY. THE TORNADO CONTINUED MOVING WEST IN THE VICINITY OF ORANGE STREET BEFORE DISSIPATING JUST BEFORE REACHING HWY 1.

THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON VILLANO BEACH NEAR 4TH AND 5TH STREET CAUSING MINOR TREE DAMAGE AS IT MOVED WEST. AFTER CROSSING THE INTER-COASTAL WATERWAY THE TORNADO INTENSIFIED TO EF-1 WITH 85-100MPH WINDS AND SNAPPED/UPROOTED MULTIPLE TREES AND CAUSED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE ALONG JACKSON AND NORTH BLVDS. THE TORNADO THEN CROSSED HWY 1 AND CAUSED ADDITIONAL TREE AND ROOF DAMAGE FROM 4TH STREET TO PORTER ROAD BEFORE DISSIPATING IN THE 12 MILE SWAMP CONSERVATION AREA.
MEDIA PASSED ON REPORT BY PUBLIC OF POSSIBLE TORNADO AT VILANO BEACH AT 4TH STREET.
MULTIPLE TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN. ROOF DAMAGE ON 5TH AVENUE NEAR VILLANO BEACH.
PUBLIC REPORT OF WIND DAMAGE. TORNADO REPORT EAST OF THIS LOCATION SO POTENTIALLY MORE TORNADO DAMAGE.

THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN NORTHERN FLAGLER COUNTY JUST SOUTH OF MARINELAND. DAMAGE WAS LIMITED TO TREES WITH A TORNADIC DEBRIS SIGNATURE NOTED ON KJAX RADAR. THE TORNADO REMAINED OVER THE PELLICER CREEK CONSERVATION AREA BEFORE DISSIPATING JUST WEST OF I-95.

THIS TORNADO CAUSED DAMAGE ON THE SOUTHERN END OF CRESCENT BEACH AT THE SUMMER HOUSE HOMES. CONSIDERABLE STRUCTURAL DAMAGE OCCURRED WITH THE REMOVAL OF MULTIPLE ROOFS AND 3RD FLOOR WALLS, SOME OF WHICH WERE INTERIOR. EXTERIOR WINDOWS IN ALL DIRECTIONS WERE ALSO BLOWN OUT. ROOF AND WINDOW DAMAGE ALSO OCCURRED AT SEVERAL HOMES ON THE NORTHERN SIDE OF THE SUMMER HOUSE COMPLEX. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED OVER THE INTER-COASTAL WATERWAY.

THIS WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE NORTHERN END OF AMELIA ISLAND AND TRACKED WESTWARD THROUGH MARSH AREAS. DAMAGE WAS LIMITED TO TREES AND SHRUBS WITH A CLEAR TORNADIC DEBRIS SIGNATURE NOTED ON KJAX RADAR. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED OVER RURAL MARSH AREAS JUST TO THE WEST.
